chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT LARKANA Const. Petition No. D- 909 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE Hearing of case. 1.For orders on office objection ‘A’ 2.For Hearing of main case. 22.10.2025. Petitioner present in person. Mr. Liaquat Ali Shar, Addl. AG along with Imran Khan, Assistant Commissioner, Warah, Bakhtiar Malik, DSP Warah and Insp. Rafique Ahmed Khero, SHO PS Warah. -------- The petitioner, Ali Muhammad Brohi, present in person, submits that he, along with other co-villagers, has jointly constructed a mosque in Village KumbDaro, Taluka Warah, District KamberShahdadkot at Kamber and has been performing duties as Pesh Imam therein. He alleges that private respondents No. 11 to 15 have unlawfully and forcibly encroached upon the said mosque and have restrained children and co-villagers from imparting and receiving Quranic and Islamic education. He further states that no remedial action has been taken despite approaching the official respondents. Pursuant to notice, respondents No. 5, 8, 9, and 11 to 15 have entered an appearance. Mr. Maqsood Ahmed Khoso, Advocate, files Vakalatnama on behalf of private respondents No. 11 to 15, which is taken on record. Learned Additional Advocate General has filed statements on behalf of official respondents No. 8 and 9, which are also taken on record. Learned counsel for the private respondents and the official respondents No. 8 and 9 categorically submit that no illegal occupation of the mosque has taken place and that children are receiving Quranic education while adults are peacefully performing Islamic obligations therein. Respondent No. 5, Assistant Commissioner, Warah, present in Court, admits that the petitioner’s application (available at Page No. 17 of the Court file) and forwarded by the Commissioner, Larkana Division, is pending in his office.

Learned counsel for the private respondents and the official respondents No. 8 and 9 categorically submit that no illegal occupation of the mosque has taken place and that children are receiving Quranic education while adults are peacefully performing Islamic obligations therein. Respondent No. 5, Assistant Commissioner, Warah, present in Court, admits that the petitioner’s application (available at Page No. 17 of the Court file) and forwarded by the Commissioner, Larkana Division, is pending in his office. He undertakes to decide the same strictly in accordance with the law. In view of the above undertaking, the petitioner expresses satisfaction and seeks the disposal of the petition. Accordingly, this petition stands disposed of with directions to respondent No. 5 to decide the petitioner’s application strictly in accordance with law, after affording an opportunity of hearing to all concerned. Judge Judge Abid H. Qazi/**
